EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui.summary: Hermes Web UI is a comprehensive management dashboard for the Hermes Agent ecosystem that centralizes AI chat, configuration, and monitoring into a single interface. It provides a unified control plane for managing multi-platform channels like Telegram, Discord, and WhatsApp while offering deep visibility into token usage, costs, and session history. The system features a built-in file browser, a web-based terminal, and a profile management system to isolate different agent configurations and backends. Users can also schedule cron jobs, manage model provider credentials, and participate in multi-agent group chats with automated context compression.
highlights: Unified configuration for 8 messaging platforms including Slack and WeChat
Real-time usage analytics for token consumption and cost tracking
Integrated file browser and web terminal for remote backend management

Web3CZ/Web3Hermes.summary: Web3Hermes is a lightweight WebUI for Hermes Agent specifically localized for Chinese-speaking users. It provides a desktop-optimized interface that simplifies interaction with the agent through a localized workflow and full Chinese language support. The platform integrates a built-in workspace file browser, session management tools, and support for multiple AI models including OpenAI, Anthropic, and DeepSeek. Users can manage tasks, edit code with syntax highlighting, and visualize tool calls within a responsive dark or light themed environment.
highlights: Localized Chinese interface with dark and light theme support
Integrated workspace file browser for direct file editing and management
Comprehensive session management including export, search, and tool call visualization

AxDSan/mnemosyne.summary: Mnemosyne is a local-first memory system designed to provide persistent storage for Hermes Agents without relying on cloud services. It utilizes a SQLite backend with native vector search and full-text search to manage conversations, preferences, and knowledge entirely on-device. The system features a three-tier architecture—working, episodic, and scratchpad—to handle different memory scales and types. By running in-process, it eliminates network latency and API costs while ensuring full data privacy and offline capability.
highlights: Local SQLite storage with native vector and full-text search
Sub-millisecond read, write, and search latency without network calls
Native Hermes Agent integration with automated episodic consolidation cycles

keepnotes-ai/keep.summary: Keep is a reflective memory system for AI agents that enables long-term learning and contextual awareness. It processes diverse inputs—including URLs, codebases, and media files—by summarizing, embedding, and organizing them into a navigable graph of "edge tags." The system automatically surfaces relevant past learnings and commitments during agent sessions to improve decision-making. It supports fully local operation via Ollama or MLX, as well as cloud-hosted and API-based configurations.
highlights: Indexes codebases, documents, and media with automatic summarization and OCR
Uses edge tags to create navigable, bidirectional relationships between notes
Provides MCP and CLI interfaces for seamless agent tool integration

AMAP-ML/SkillClaw.summary: SkillClaw is an agentic skill management system that enables AI agents to evolve their capabilities through real-world interactions. It operates as a background process that automatically deduplicates, improves, and merges skills across different sessions and devices. The system creates a unified skill library that synchronizes experience between multiple agents and users, allowing knowledge gained in one context to benefit others. It natively integrates with Hermes, OpenClaw, and various OpenAI-compatible APIs to provide a continuous evolution loop for agentic tools.
highlights: Automates skill deduplication and quality improvement across agent sessions
Unifies skill libraries across multiple devices and different agent frameworks
Enables collective evolution by sharing learned skills within user groups

aivrar/portable-hermes-agent.summary: Portable Hermes Agent is a standalone Windows desktop application that provides a graphical interface for the Hermes Agent framework without requiring Docker, administrator rights, or system-wide installations. It operates through an embedded Python environment and connects to local models via LM Studio or cloud providers like OpenRouter. The platform integrates over 100 tools for tasks including image generation via ComfyUI, text-to-speech, music creation, and automated multi-step workflows. Users can also extend the agent's capabilities at runtime using a built-in dynamic tool maker for API wrappers and Python handlers.
highlights: Zero-install Windows desktop GUI with granular permission controls
Over 100 tools including TTS, MusicGen, and ComfyUI integrations
Dynamic tool maker for creating API wrappers at runtime

iamagenius00/hermes-a2a.summary: hermes-a2a is a plugin for Hermes Agent that implements Google's A2A protocol to enable direct peer-to-peer communication between autonomous agents. It works by injecting external messages into an agent's active session via HMAC-signed webhooks, allowing for instant responses without polling or session cloning. This architecture ensures agents maintain a single shared memory with their user while collaborating on tasks like debugging, status reporting, or peer consultation. The system includes built-in security features such as prompt injection filtering, outbound redaction, and independent conversation storage to prevent data loss during context compaction.
highlights: Enables peer-to-peer agent communication without manual user relaying
Injects messages into active sessions to maintain consistent agent memory
Triggers instant agent responses via HMAC-signed webhook architecture

Cranot/super-hermes.summary: Super Hermes is a skill registry for the Hermes Agent that enables models to generate their own analytical frameworks, or prisms, for deep code and document inspection. It works by instructing the agent to write specific thinking instructions before execution, allowing it to derive structural trade-offs and conservation laws rather than just identifying surface-level bugs. The system includes specialized commands like /prism-reflect, which provide a constraint report detailing what the analysis missed to ensure transparency in the agent's cognitive limits.
highlights: Teaches agents to write custom thinking instructions for specific problems
Identifies structural conservation laws and trade-offs in complex codebases
Generates constraint reports detailing analysis blind spots and limitations

EfficientContext/ContextPilot.summary: ContextPilot is a context optimization engine designed to accelerate long-context LLM inference by maximizing prefix cache reuse. It functions by maintaining a context index that reorders and deduplicates overlapping data blocks before they reach the inference server. This approach reduces redundant KV cache recomputation and lowers token consumption without compromising reasoning accuracy. The project provides native plugins for Hermes Agent and OpenClaw, and supports backends like vLLM, SGLang, and llama.cpp.
highlights: Reduces prompt tokens by up to 36% through context deduplication
Boosts prefill speeds by 1.5–3× via optimized prefix sharing
Native integration for Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and vLLM

335234131/agent-browser-mcp.summary: Agent-browser-mcp is a Model Context Protocol server that enables AI agents to interact directly with a user's active Chrome browser session rather than a sandboxed environment. It works through a local bridge and a Chrome extension to maintain existing login states, cookies, and open tabs while providing low-level control. The project allows agents to perform page scanning, execute JavaScript, and issue Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) commands. It also supports physical hardware interaction, including real mouse movements, keyboard inputs, and desktop screenshots.
highlights: Operates real Chrome sessions with preserved login states and cookies
Executes native CDP commands for advanced DOM and browser control
Supports physical mouse and keyboard inputs for human-like automation

jwangkun/hermes-agent-guide.summary: The Hermes Agent Guide is a comprehensive Chinese-language documentation project designed to bridge the gap between fragmented information and practical implementation of the Hermes Agent framework. It provides a structured 16-book knowledge map covering the system's five-layer architecture, three-tier memory systems, and the integration of over 47 built-in tools. The guide details technical workflows for local and cloud deployment, MCP protocol automation, and multi-platform connectivity with services like WeChat and Discord. Users can follow specialized learning paths for development, commercial monetization, or migrating from the OpenClaw framework.
highlights: 16-book systematic guide with over 300,000 words of technical content
Detailed analysis of five-layer architecture and three-tier memory systems
Step-by-step integration for 15+ platforms including Feishu and Slack

longyunfeigu/learn-hermes-agent.summary: Learn Hermes Agent is a 27-chapter hands-on tutorial for building production-grade autonomous AI agents from scratch using Python. The curriculum guides developers through implementing a core agent loop, tool registration, session persistence with SQLite, and context compression. It covers advanced infrastructure including multi-platform gateways for Telegram and Discord, Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration, and RL-based self-evolution. The project provides runnable reference implementations for each stage to help users understand the design backbone of autonomous systems.
highlights: Step-by-step implementation of agent loops and tool dispatch systems
Multi-platform gateway supporting Telegram, Discord, Slack, and WeChat
Advanced memory management with context compression and session persistence

jpalmae/hermeshq.summary: HermesHQ is a Docker-first control plane designed to orchestrate and manage multiple Hermes Agent instances from a single web application. It functions as an operational layer that wraps the Hermes execution engine, providing centralized task dispatching, scheduling, and a runtime ledger. The platform enables multi-agent delegation, RBAC-controlled user access, and per-agent communication channels for Telegram and WhatsApp. It also features an enterprise MCP bridge to expose specific agents to external AI clients like Claude Code and Codex.
highlights: Orchestrates multiple Hermes Agent instances with separate workspaces and identities
Provides hierarchy-aware inter-agent delegation and task scheduling
Offers enterprise MCP access for external AI client integrations

plastic-labs/honcho.summary: Honcho is an open-source memory library and managed service designed to build stateful agents that maintain long-term context about users, groups, and ideas. It functions as a continual learning system that captures interaction history and applies reasoning to derive insights about entities as they evolve over time. The library provides SDKs for Python and TypeScript, allowing developers to implement session-scoped representations and natural language querying of agent memory. By integrating with various LLM providers, it enables features like automatic conversation summarization, vector search for message history, and context formatting for model completions.
highlights: Maintains persistent state for users, agents, and evolving entities
Provides natural language querying of interaction history and user traits
Supports Python and TypeScript SDKs with managed or self-hosted options

adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud.summary: This project is a Hermes Agent skill that enables direct interaction with self-hosted Nextcloud instances for personal data management. It interfaces with Nextcloud using WebDAV, CalDAV, CardDAV, and the Notes API to perform operations without requiring a browser or third-party sync services. Users can manage files, edit notes, schedule calendar events, track tasks, and search contacts through a command-line interface. The skill prioritizes security by utilizing Nextcloud App Passwords and storing credentials in a restricted local environment file.
highlights: Manages files, notes, calendars, tasks, and contacts via standard protocols.
Communicates securely over HTTPS using Nextcloud App Passwords.
Includes a guided setup script for credential validation and configuration.
